Dear Team,

I hope this message finds you well.

I would like to inform everyone that Sir Sam will be available starting at 5 PM today.

Documents for Signing:
If there are any documents that require Sir Sam's signature, please ensure they are sent by tomorrow morning.

Resend Feedback/Approval Requests:
If anyone has requested Sir Sam's feedback or approval in the last 9 days and has not received a reply, please resend your requests by this evening or tomorrow morning. To prioritize, please add "For Feedback" or "For Approval" to your subject line.

Thank you for your attention to these matte

I will execute the SSS action plan if Drei will still be unresponsive over the weekend. All SSS matters had already been discussed with Mam Mae yesterday morning, I also told Mam Mae that everything is in email he hasnt responded yet esp with SSS access.
I already called SSS and they asked me to write a letter signed by the President, stating that I am the new representative then they will process so I can have access.

SSS Issues: (#1 issue) Maternity Claims - Drei failed to notify SSS of our 2 pregnant employees. Upon checking with SSS, no notifications done. 1 Employee has now given birth and employer is required to pay employee in advance of the SSS maternity claims, however, before I make the request for payment, I need to make sure that it has been filed to SSS so we can collect from SSS which will usually take 2-6 months (#2 issue) Some Employees availed an SSS loan awaiting online approval from employer so SSS can process, employer approval is through SSS Employer portal, however, some employees claim

We are pleased to welcome Mr. @Peter John Lesaca to our team as a new Business Development Manager. Mr. Lesaca has a robust background and extensive experience in various retail functions, including Operations, Sales, Marketing, Inventory, Customer Service, Store and People Management, and Business Development, gained from organizations both locally and internationally.

Hi Sir @Sam Karazi We will revise the form to include locked formulas and necessary restrictions to ensure accuracy and reduce manual data entry. Additionally, your suggestion to create a webform that automatically transfers data to the report is excellent. will coordinate with the developer to implement this solution.and we will ensure that only authorized personnel have editing access to the final report, while our cashiers and support team will have restricted access.
Thank you for highlighting these issues, and we will work on making the necessary improvements.

@Lenie Zaleta I noticed that the form you sent me is all manual, no locked formulas no restrictions. This is not what I had in mind, having all the report manually entered including the averages, month to date etc... can result in a lot of human error. That info can be easily collected by creating formulas. 
I asked my developer to create a webform that our team will have to fill daily the form will collect all the important information and transfer that data automatically to the report. Our cashiers and support shouldn't have editing access to the final report.
Thank you
